citation_short: ICRC/ICTY casebook on Prosecutor v. Galić
title: ICTY, Prosecutor v. Galić (ICRC case study)
pages_or_sections: Online casebook lines discussing deliberate attacks on civilians, confrontation-line deaths, and Sarajevo population/troop figures
strengths: Strong legal attribution and explicit figures for Sarajevo inhabitants, in-city troop presence, and civilian deaths.
limitations: Window is indictment-period only, and the non-civilian remainder is not fully disaggregated by status.
notes: Key extracts used: deliberate attacks on civilians; Markale shelling from SRK-controlled territory; minimum 3,798 killed including 1,399 civilians; ~45,000 troops and ~340,000 inhabitants.

title: Prlić et al. Indictment
pages_or_sections: Paragraphs on East Mostar population and HVO shelling/sniping at civilians
strengths: Directly identifies East Mostar's Muslim population and HVO attacks on civilians.
limitations: The 135 figure is killed-or-wounded, not deaths alone, so it cannot be converted into a death total.
notes: Used for side-attributed civilian casualty claim and East Mostar civilian population input.

citation_short: Tabeau, East Mostar siege-related deaths report
title: Prlić et al. / Mostar siege-related deaths on the territory of East Mostar
author_or_org: ICTY Office of the Prosecutor expert report
pages_or_sections: Summary / Table 5 and related summary language on minimum deaths and civilian/combatant split
strengths: Dedicated demographic death compilation for the East Mostar siege.
limitations: Accessible extract clearly supports total siege deaths; the 49.5% civilian / 50.5% combatant split is taken from source summary language rather than a single verified page line in the accessible extract.
notes: Search-result summary stated that a minimum of 539 persons died in East Mostar, of whom 49.5% were civilians and 50.5% combatants.

title: Srebrenica Genocide 20th Anniversary special
pages_or_sections: Opening summary lines on executed victims and enclave population
strengths: Tribunal-linked summary clearly identifies Bosnian Serb forces as perpetrators and Bosnian Muslim men and boys as victims.
limitations: The source gives aggregate men-and-boys killing figures without fully classifying each victim as civilian or combatant.
notes: Used conservatively as a floor (more than 7,000) rather than forcing an 8,000 point estimate from outside the retained source chain.

title: Stakić / Prijedor victim estimate expert report
author_or_org: ICTY Office of the Prosecutor expert report
pages_or_sections: Opening summary lines on killed-or-missing totals and victim-community composition
strengths: Provides a municipality-scale capture-recapture estimate and explicit victim-community composition.
limitations: The core aggregate is killed-or-missing, not a clean direct-death count, so it cannot be promoted into a publishable direct mortality estimate without additional confirmation.
notes: Key figures used: 2,190 persons killed or missing; 1,651 of 1,731 observed victims were Muslims (95.4%).
